vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1797: One-time harness re-default migration: deacon resume of pre-1787 agents should re-resolve harness instead of keeping stale pairings

plan.title: One-time harness re-default migration: deacon resume of pre-1787 agents should re-resolve harness instead of keeping stale pairings
plan.author: agent:claude-fable-5

references.label: PRD draft with verified file:line references and before/after snippets

narratives.Problem: resumeAgent() passes the stored agentState.harness to resolveHarness() as `explicit` (src/lib/agents.ts:4655-4659), which always wins over role/provider/built-in defaults. Pre-1787 agents also lack session-origin metadata, so sessionResumeDriftReasons() returns [] (agents.ts:2164) and --resume is kept. A system restart therefore respawns the whole pre-1787 fleet with exactly the stale model/harness pairings (e.g. gpt-5.5 + claude-code instead of codex) that PAN-1787's built-in defaults exist to fix. Additionally, resumeAgent never writes session-origin metadata after launching a fresh session (only spawnRun:3336 and spawnAgent:3687 do), so PAN-1787 drift respawns repeat on every subsequent resume — the 'one-time by construction' property does not currently hold for resume-launched fresh sessions.
narratives.Proposal: Inside resumeAgent() only: when the caller passed no explicit harness AND the runtime snapshot lacks sessionModel/sessionHarness (legacy predicate, agents.ts:2164), call resolveHarness({ role, model }) with NO explicit key. If the result differs from agentState.harness ?? 'claude-code', adopt it, push a 'legacy harness migration old→new' reason onto the existing resumeDriftReasons list (agents.ts:4668) so all PAN-1787 fresh-session plumbing fires unchanged (no --resume, fresh session id, standard continue.json re-onboarding prompt, kickoff-style delivery), and log one migration line per agent. If equal, keep --resume (PAN-1787 backward-compat AC preserved). Separately, extend the post-launch saveAgentRuntimeState call (agents.ts:4807-4810) to persist sessionModel/sessionHarness on every fresh respawn, making migration and drift one-time by construction. Exact before/after snippets are in .pan/drafts/PAN-1797.md.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No change to restartAgent (operator-initiated; can pass --harness explicitly)
- No change to spawnRun/spawnAgent origin writes (already correct)
- No bulk offline rewrite of state.json files — migration is lazy, at resume time
- No distinction between 'explicitly chosen' and 'old default' legacy harnesses (accepted tradeoff per issue; re-pin via Settings/role overrides after migration)
- No config flag or kill-switch for the migration (one-time by construction)
- No dashboard UI changes
- No behavior change for agents WITH origin metadata (PAN-1787 drift rules untouched)

autoDecisions.summary: Implement the migration in resumeAgent() (shared path) rather than deacon-only code.
autoDecisions.rationale: The deacon calls resumeAgent(agentId) with no opts (deacon.ts:6434), so the deacon path is covered, and manual no-harness resumes of legacy agents migrate identically instead of keeping stale pairings. Caller-explicit opts.harness (dashboard picker) bypasses the migration, preserving operator intent.
States with no harness field predate PAN-636 and actually ran the old claude-code launcher default, so claude-code is what the session was started with.

autoDecisions.summary: Fold the migration into the existing drift-reasons machinery instead of a parallel branch.
autoDecisions.rationale: All fresh-session plumbing (session id handling, message selection, delivery, logging) already keys off resumeDriftReasons/shouldResumeSavedSession; reusing it guarantees behavioral parity with PAN-1787 drift and minimizes new control flow.

autoDecisions.summary: Write session-origin metadata on ALL resumeAgent fresh respawns (drift, compact, migration), not only the migration path.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue's stated mechanism is 'the fresh session writes origin metadata, after which PAN-1787's normal rules govern' — currently false for resumeAgent-launched fresh sessions. The reducer's '?? prev' semantics (event-reducers.ts:1088-1096) otherwise keep stale origin, making drifted agents fresh-respawn on every subsequent resume.

autoDecisions.summary: No config flag/kill-switch; no docs page update.
autoDecisions.rationale: The migration is one-time by construction (origin metadata + converged state.harness); a flag would outlive its purpose. The per-agent migration log line is the operational audit trail.

autoDecisions.summary: Test models: gpt-5.5 (→ codex) and kimi-k2.5 (→ pi) with stub binaries on PATH.
autoDecisions.rationale: anthropic→claude-code, openai→codex, others→pi (providers.ts:67-178); stub-binary PATH manipulation plus resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests() is the established deterministic pattern (agent-spawning.test.ts:917-923).

items.title: Re-resolve harness in resumeAgent for legacy sessions lacking origin metadata

narrative.Action: In resumeAgent() (src/lib/agents.ts), replace the harness resolution at lines 4655-4660 per the exact before/after snippet in .pan/drafts/PAN-1797.md § Proposal: compute storedHarness = agentState.harness ?? 'claude-code', isLegacySessionOrigin = !runtimeState?.sessionModel || !runtimeState?.sessionHarness, migrateLegacyHarness = opts?.harness === undefined && isLegacySessionOrigin; call resolveHarness WITHOUT the explicit key when migrating (otherwise keep explicit: opts?.harness ?? agentState.harness); compute legacyMigrationReason = 'legacy harness migration <old>→<new>' when migrating and the result differs from storedHarness. After the sessionResumeDriftReasons call at line 4668, push legacyMigrationReason onto resumeDriftReasons and emit exactly one migration log line via console.log and logAgentLifecycleSync naming old harness, new harness, and model. All downstream fresh-session plumbing (shouldResumeSavedSession, freshSessionId, session.id unlink, defaultResumeMessage, kickoff-style delivery, fresh-respawn logging at 4800-4802) is reused unchanged. The existing agentState.harness = effectiveHarness (4660) and codexMode handling (4661-4665) already persist the adopted harness.
narrative.Why: Stored state.harness passed as `explicit` always wins (harness-resolve.ts:69), freezing pre-1787 stale pairings forever; legacy agents also never trip drift (agents.ts:2164), so --resume preserves the bad pairing across every restart.

items.title: Given a stopped agent with no session-origin metadata, state harness claude-code, and model gpt-5.5 with a codex binary present, when resumeAgent runs with no opts, then the launcher contains no --resume, state.json harness updates to codex, and codexMode updates to work-tui
Given the same legacy agent, when the migration fires, then the agent lifecycle log records exactly one line containing 'legacy harness migration claude-code→codex' and the model id

Given a legacy agent whose model re-resolves to its stored harness (anthropic model + claude-code), when resumeAgent runs with no opts, then it preserves --resume with the saved session id (existing test at tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:621 passes unmodified)

Given a legacy agent resumed with caller-explicit opts.harness, when resumeAgent runs, then resolveHarness receives that harness as explicit and the migration does not fire (--resume preserved absent drift)

Given an agent WITH session-origin metadata, when resumeAgent runs, then resolution still passes explicit: opts?.harness ?? agentState.harness and PAN-1787 drift behavior is preserved (existing drift tests at agent-spawning.test.ts:577-619 pass unmodified)
Persist session-origin metadata on resumeAgent fresh respawns

narrative.Action: In resumeAgent() (src/lib/agents.ts), extend the existing post-launch saveAgentRuntimeState call at lines 4807-4810 to additionally include sessionModel: model and sessionHarness: effectiveHarness when !shouldResumeSavedSession (i.e. any fresh respawn: compact recovery, PAN-1787 drift, or PAN-1797 migration). Both fields must be written in the same patch — the model_set reducer keeps prior values via '?? prev' (packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts:1088-1096), so a single-field write would half-update origin. Normal --resume relaunches must NOT write origin fields. Exact snippet in .pan/drafts/PAN-1797.md § Proposal.
narrative.Why: resumeAgent never writes origin metadata today (only spawnRun:3336 and spawnAgent:3687 do), so drift/migration respawns leave stale origin in place and re-trigger a fresh session on every subsequent resume; this write makes them one-time by construction, as the issue requires.

items.title: Given resumeAgent launches a fresh session (drift or migration), then it emits a model_set event carrying both sessionModel equal to the resumed model and sessionHarness equal to the effective harness
Given resumeAgent re-attaches with --resume (no drift, no migration), then it emits no model_set event containing sessionModel or sessionHarness fields (prior origin preserved)
Integration tests for legacy harness migration in agent-spawning.test.ts

narrative.Action: Add integration tests to tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts next to the PAN-1787 drift tests (lines 577-631). Extend writeResumableWorkAgent (line 344) with optional model/harness overrides. Use the stub-binary PATH pattern (lines 917-923: write executable '#!/bin/sh\nexit 0' stub into a temp bin dir, prepend to process.env.PATH, then call resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests()). Tests: (1) legacy origin (bare setRuntimeOrigin(agentId)) + state.model gpt-5.5 + stub codex binary → resumeAgent(agentId) yields launcher without --resume, state.json harness codex, codexMode work-tui, lifecycle log contains the migration line; (2) legacy + state.model kimi-k2.5 + stub pi binary + process.env.KIMI_API_KEY set (delete in finally, pattern at line 326) → fresh session with harness pi; (3) the existing legacy test at line 621 (anthropic model → equal) passes unmodified; (4) legacy + kimi model + explicit opts.harness 'claude-code' → no migration, launcher preserves --resume. Note: cliproxy is already mocked running for gpt-5.5; PANOPTICON_PROMPT_READY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=1 is already set in beforeEach — do not add real-time waits (fake-timers rule).
narrative.Why: The migration changes fleet-wide restart behavior; launcher-level assertions (presence/absence of --resume, --session-id) are the observable contract the deacon path relies on, and binary stubbing keeps resolution deterministic across machines/CI.

items.title: Given a legacy gpt-5.5 agent with a stubbed codex binary, when resumed with no opts, then the test asserts the launcher contains no --resume, state.json harness equals codex, and the lifecycle log contains the migration line
Given a legacy kimi-k2.5 agent with a stubbed pi binary, when resumed with no opts, then the test asserts a fresh session launches with harness pi persisted in state.json

Given the existing PAN-1787 legacy backward-compat test (line 621), when the full suite runs, then it passes unmodified, and a new explicit-harness test asserts --resume is preserved when opts.harness is supplied for a legacy agent
Given any in-test PATH mutation, then the test calls resetHarnessResolveCachesForTests() before resolution so binary availability caching survives across tests deterministically
Unit tests for resume-path origin writes and non-explicit resolver call

narrative.Action: Add resumeAgent-path tests to src/lib/__tests__/agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts, which already mocks resolveHarness (resolveHarnessMock) and emitAgentEvent (emitAgentEventMock) — follow the origin-write assertion patterns at lines 331-420. Set up a stopped resumable agent (agent dir with state.json status 'stopped', session.id file, initial-prompt.md, existing workspace dir — mirror the writeResumableWorkAgent shape from tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:344-366, plus a runtime snapshot mock for origin state). Tests: (1) fresh respawn after origin drift emits a model_set event with expect.objectContaining({ sessionModel: <new model>, sessionHarness: <new harness> }); (2) a matching-origin --resume emits no model_set call having sessionModel/sessionHarness properties (use the mock.calls.find pattern at lines 418-420); (3) a legacy resume (snapshot without origin fields) calls resolveHarness without an explicit key — assert the call argument object has no 'explicit' own-property (or explicit: undefined, matching the chosen implementation from the legacy-harness-re-resolution bead).
narrative.Why: The integration suite asserts launcher behavior, but the event-level contract (both origin fields written together, never on plain --resume, resolver called without explicit) is only observable through the mocked emitAgentEvent/resolveHarness seams this file already provides.

items.title: Given a drift-triggered fresh respawn through resumeAgent, then the test asserts emitAgentEvent receives a model_set event containing both sessionModel and sessionHarness for the new session
Given a normal --resume relaunch with matching origin, then the test asserts no model_set call contains sessionModel or sessionHarness properties
Given a legacy agent resumed with no opts, then the test asserts resolveHarness receives no explicit harness (role and model only)
